notes: BURNDOWN PARSE — stop the 60s false-timeout on EDPs with no real job list. STACKS ON v1.6.79 (Resolve) + v1.6.80 (multi-site ship A): deploy in order 79 -> 80 -> 81; each is a superset so an in-order deploy makes each gate see only its own diff. Change: the burndown section's skipIf gate was firing the heavy 60s AI extraction whenever ANY of a loose keyword list appeared — including incidental words like 'ticket', 'in progress', 'wip', 'remediation' — which then timed out on slow networks even when the EDP had no job list. skipIf is now a strong/weak scorer: STRONG tokens (burndown, work order, wo-, job id, task/punch list, cabling/fiber job) fire the run on any single hit; WEAK incidental tokens (ticket, fs#, jira, in progress, wip, remediation) require 2+ distinct hits to corroborate before spending the call; otherwise the section is skipped cleanly (marked 'no relevant content found'). RETRY still force-runs past the gate (edp_retrySection bypasses skipIf), so a real job list described only in weak words is one tap away. No change to the 60s timeout, the prompt, or extraction. Single-function edit. Brace heuristic 3121/3121 BALANCED (carried from v1.6.80; no CSS touched; node --check clean). Three stamps at v1.6.81.
